name: Ibaigorri Trail
name_original: Lerín, Allo, Antiguo Poblado de Baigorri – Ruta Circular
description: This difficult 30.3-mile touring cycling route near Lerín offers diverse landscapes, historical sites, and 1769 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Lerín are set within the diverse landscapes of Navarra, Spain. The region features a strategic hilltop location offering panoramic views of the surrounding interior, including the Ebro River and extensive vineyards. Gypsum and limestone mountain ranges are traversed by the Ega River, contributing to varied terrain. Pine forests, such as the Pinares de Lerín, also provide natural resources and signposted routes suitable for cycling. Best touring cycling routes around Lerín The most popular touring cycling route is Ibaigorri Trail, a 30.3 miles (48.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 36 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive exploration of the local landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Miranda de Arga - Circular Route, an easy 3.4 miles (5.4 km) path. This shorter route provides a gentle ride through the area around Miranda de Arga. Local touring cyclists also love the Mirador de la Peña – Views of Lerín loop from Lerín, a 23.2 miles (37.4 km) trail leading through areas with panoramic hilltop views, often completed in about 3 hours 41 minutes. Touring cycling around Lerín is defined by hilltop views, river valleys, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy circular routes to more challenging long-distance trails. The routes in Lerín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from 5 reviews. More than 250 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lerín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Lerín?
A: There are over 45 touring cycling routes around Lerín. These routes cater to various skill levels, with a good mix of moderate and challenging options, alongside a few easier paths for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Lerín?
A: Yes, for an easier ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e972761467/miranda-de-arga-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miranda de Arga - Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;. This 5.4 km path offers a gentle introduction to touring cycling in the area, perfect for those seeking a less strenuous experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Lerín?
A: Lerín's touring cycling routes feature diverse terrain. You'll encounter strategic hilltop locations offering panoramic views, river valleys carved by the Ega River, and extensive vineyards. The region also includes pine forests, such as the Pinares de Lerín, which offer signposted routes suitable for cycling.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views or viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. Lerín is known for its spectacular views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Peña – Views of Lerín loop from Lerín&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, leading through areas with panoramic hilltop vistas. The &lt;strong&gt;Mirador de la Peña&lt;/strong&gt; itself is a standout viewpoint, offering beautiful scenery and excellent conditions for stargazing. You can also enjoy scenic views of the Ebro River and vineyards from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5630898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;rest area near the Santa Cruz hermitage&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I explore along the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Lerín offers historical points of interest. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5125275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baigorri Ruins&lt;/a&gt;, once properties of the Counts of Lerín, or the well-preserved &lt;strong&gt;Fuerte Cazorla&lt;/strong&gt;, a significant site from the Carlist Wars. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5674457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lodosa&lt;/a&gt;, known for its piquillo peppers, is also nearby along the Ebro River.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Lerín?
A: The touring cycling routes around Lerín are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.25 stars from 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from challenging climbs to scenic river valleys, and the rewarding panoramic views.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling options near Lerín?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, the broader Navarra region features an extensive network of 'Greenways' (Vías Verdes), which are repurposed disused railway lines ideal for cycling. Additionally, a significant portion of &lt;strong&gt;EuroVelo 1&lt;/strong&gt;, a long-distance route connecting North Cape to Sagres, passes through Navarra, offering opportunities for extensive touring.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Lerín are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38171374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Peña loop from Cárcar&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging 63.8 km circular ride, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e972761467/miranda-de-arga-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miranda de Arga - Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; provides an easier, shorter loop.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Lerín?
A: While specific weather data isn't provided, the region's diverse landscapes, including river valleys and pine forests, suggest pleasant conditions for cycling during spring and autumn. These seasons typically offer milder temperatures, making longer rides more comfortable and allowing you to fully appreciate the natural beauty without extreme heat.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the pine forests mentioned in the region?
A: Yes, the local council has signposted six naturalistic routes within the &lt;strong&gt;Pinares de Lerín&lt;/strong&gt; (Pine Forests of Lerín). These forests are explicitly recommended for cycling and offer a significant natural resource for exploration, providing a different kind of scenery compared to the open hilltop views.

Q: What is the most challenging touring cycling route around Lerín?
A: Among the more challenging options, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e972853811/ibaigorri-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibaigorri Trail&lt;/a&gt; stands out. This difficult 48.7 km route involves significant elevation changes, providing an extensive exploration of the local landscape for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: Can I find routes that follow river valleys?
A: Yes, the municipal area of Lerín is traversed by the Ega River, which cuts through gypsum and limestone mountain ranges. Many touring cycling routes will follow these river valleys, offering scenic views and often gentler gradients compared to the hilltop climbs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31895446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Fountain – Ermita de San Miguel loop from Berbinzana&lt;/a&gt; includes sections that traverse varied terrain, likely incorporating riverine landscapes.
tips: They say that Lerín is the town in Spain where the stars shine the most, thanks to very little light pollution. An ideal place to contemplate the starry skies.

Medieval hermitage in the middle of cultivated fields, at the gates of the Sierra de Arosia. It is a spectacular landscape and the place is especially quiet. It doesn't have a fountain or tables, but it's nice.
It was one of the historical properties of the Counts of Lerín and the Duke of Alba. Half a tower resists the passage of time and the winds and rains.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle the Lerín, Allo, Antiguo Poblado de Baigorri – Circular Route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is approximately 48.7 kilometers long and typically takes around 3 hours and 36 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling speed and stops along the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route traverses the diverse landscape of Navarra, offering a mix of perfectly surfaced roads and potentially rougher tracks or farmways. You can expect varied scenery, including wooded areas, valleys, and agricultural fields. Some sections might be more suitable for bikes with wider tires, though it's primarily a touring bicycle route.

faqs.question: Is the Lerín, Allo, Antiguo Poblado de Baigorri – Circular Route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is graded as 'difficult' due to its length and an elevation gain of approximately 539 meters. While some parts might be gentle, the overall challenge makes it less suitable for absolute beginners. Cyclists with some experience and good fitness will find it more enjoyable.

faqs.question: What historical sites or viewpoints can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several interesting points. Keep an eye out for the Mirador de la Peña, which offers great views. The route also passes by the Hermitage of Santa Cecilia of Arróniz and the Baigorri Ruins, which are historical sites.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Navarra region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, especially in July and August, while winter might bring colder weather and potentially less predictable conditions. These shoulder seasons provide the best balance for enjoying the scenery and comfortable cycling.

faqs.question: Where does the route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: As this appears to be a custom circular route linking Lerín, Allo, and Antiguo Poblado de Baigorri, there isn't one designated official trailhead. You can typically start and park in any of the towns along the route, such as Lerín or Allo. Look for public parking areas within these villages.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions mentioned for dogs on this particular route, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through villages, agricultural areas, or near roads. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It primarily uses public roads and paths in the Navarra region.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, parts of this route overlap with other paths. Approximately 16% of the route follows the Sendero Histórico: Navarra, and a small section (1%) coincides with the Acedo - Lodosa trail. You'll also briefly cross the Senda del Pajero and Calle Ramón y Cajal.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the 'touringbicycle' classification and the varied terrain described (surfaced roads and potentially rougher tracks), a sturdy touring bike or a gravel bike would be ideal. These bikes offer a good balance of comfort for longer distances and durability for mixed surfaces.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near several towns like Lerín and Allo, where you can find local cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. These villages offer opportunities to experience local culture and cuisine during your trip.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Santa Cecilia of Arróniz
